name: Fuji Apple Harvest Japan
desc: The world-famous crisp and honey-sweet apple variety originally developed in Fujisaki. A staple winter fruit in Japan.

name: Amaou Luxury Strawberry Japan
desc: Large, round, and incredibly sweet strawberries from Fukuoka. Japan is known for producing some of the world's most expensive berries.

name: Crown Melon Shizuoka Japan
desc: Exquisite muskmelons grown with extreme care. Often sold as high-end luxury gifts in department stores across Japan.

name: Satsuma Mikan Mandarin Japan
desc: Seedless and easy-to-peel citrus fruits. The quintessential winter snack often enjoyed while sitting at a Kotatsu in Japan.

name: Okayama White Peach Japan
desc: Prized for its pale skin, soft texture, and fragrant juice. A legendary summer delicacy associated with the Momotaro folk tale in Japan.

name: Shine Muscat Grape Japan
desc: Large, seedless green grapes with a crisp skin and floral honey flavor. A highly sought-after premium fruit variety in Japan.

name: Nashi Asian Pear Japan
desc: Round like an apple with a grainy, watery, and refreshing texture. A popular autumn fruit served chilled in Japan.

name: Yuzu Aromatic Citrus Japan
desc: A tart and incredibly fragrant citrus used for zest, juice, and even winter bathing rituals in Japan.

name: Sato Nishiki Cherry Japan
desc: The 'King of Cherries' from Yamagata Prefecture, known for its bright red color and perfect sweet-tart balance in Japan.

name: Momotaro Sweet Tomato Japan
desc: A pinkish-red tomato variety bred for its high sugar content and durability. A favorite for fresh salads in Japan.

name: Taiyo no Tamago Mango Japan
desc: Meaning 'Egg of the Sun,' these Miyazaki mangoes are strictly graded for sugar content and weight in Japan.

name: Shodoshima Olive Grove Japan
desc: Japan's primary olive-growing region located on the 'Olive Island' in the Seto Inland Sea of Japan.
